app_info.subtitle: Post to social media
app_info.description: SimplePost helps users work with their connected social media accounts directly from ChatGPT: list available accounts, upload generated or attached media, validate drafts on request, preview post targets and timing, publish immediately, schedule posts for later, save posts as drafts, and manage drafts or future scheduled posts. The app replies in plain text and does not render a custom widget.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves the authenticated user's connected SimplePost account metadata.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not change public internet state or external third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, publish, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Downloads a user-provided ChatGPT file parameter to the authenticated user's SimplePost storage and returns a new media URL.
justifications.open_world_justification: Creates a publicly fetchable media URL that can be used outside ChatGPT and by social platforms when posting.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, revoke access, publish a post, or perform an irreversible platform action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Checks draft text and media URLs against platform validation rules without creating records or posting.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not write to public internet state or external third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, publish, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Resolves targets, media count, scheduled time when applicable, and validation status without creating or publishing a post.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not write to SimplePost, public internet state, or external third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, publish, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a SimplePost post record as a draft, scheduled post, or immediate post and may update its status after attempting platform posting.
justifications.open_world_justification: Can publish user-confirmed content to connected external social platforms, schedule it for later publication, or store it as a SimplePost draft.
justifications.destructive_justification: Immediate posting sends public social content to external platforms, which may not be reversible through this app.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves the authenticated user's SimplePost post records and connected account metadata.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish content, update public internet state, or call external social platform write APIs.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, publish, revoke access, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Updates an existing draft or future scheduled SimplePost post record and validates the result when scheduling.
justifications.open_world_justification: Can change content or timing that SimplePost will later publish to connected external social platforms.
justifications.destructive_justification: Can overwrite draft or scheduled post content, targets, media, thread segments, scheduled time, or draft/scheduled status.

justifications.read_only_justification: Deletes a draft or future scheduled SimplePost post record and best-effort deletes its stored media.
justifications.open_world_justification: Can remove publicly fetchable SimplePost media URLs and prevents scheduled content from being published externally.
justifications.destructive_justification: Permanently deletes the selected draft or scheduled post record and stored media from SimplePost.

test_cases.description: List connected social accounts before starting a posting workflow.
test_cases.user_prompt: Which SimplePost accounts can I post from?
test_cases.expected_output: Returns text and structured JSON with the user's connected accounts, account IDs, platform names, and display metadata for selecting targets.

test_cases.description: Validate a text-only draft against selected accounts.
test_cases.user_prompt: Check whether this post is valid for my connected X and LinkedIn accounts: Shipping the new release notes today.
test_cases.expected_output: Reports in text and structured JSON whether the draft is valid, includes the exact draft post content, and surfaces any platform-specific errors or warnings.

test_cases.description: Preview a scheduled post with a public media URL.
test_cases.user_prompt: Preview this for my Instagram account tomorrow at 9 AM Berlin time using https://schedule.simplepost.dev/simplepost-logo.png: New product photos are live.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns text and structured JSON with resolved target account details, exact post content, media count, scheduled time with timezone, and validation results without creating the post.

test_cases.description: Upload an attached ChatGPT image when no public URL is available.
test_cases.user_prompt: Upload the attached image as launch.png so I can attach it to a SimplePost draft.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a publicly fetchable SimplePost media URL for an image named launch.png, including file type, filename, size, and MIME type, ready to attach to a post via the media field.

test_cases.description: Schedule a confirmed post for later publication.
test_cases.user_prompt: Schedule the confirmed post 'Office hours start at 16:00' for my X account tomorrow at 16:00 Europe/Berlin.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates a scheduled SimplePost post after internal validation, then returns the exact scheduled content, scheduled status, and posting summary.

test_cases.description: Save a confirmed post as a draft without scheduling or publishing it.
test_cases.user_prompt: Save this as a SimplePost draft for my LinkedIn account: We are preparing the launch announcement, but do not publish or schedule it yet.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates a SimplePost draft without publishing or scheduling it, then returns the exact draft content, target account details, draft status, and posting summary.

test_cases.description: Inspect drafts and future scheduled posts before making changes.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show me my SimplePost drafts and posts that are currently scheduled.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns SimplePost posts with IDs, status, target account metadata, exact content, media count, thread count, and scheduled time when present, and the answer highlights the drafts and future scheduled posts. The tool may be called once for all statuses or separately for drafts and scheduled posts.

test_cases.description: Create a temporary draft and move it to the scheduled queue.
test_cases.user_prompt: Save a temporary SimplePost draft for my LinkedIn account with text 'Review scheduling draft', then schedule that same draft for tomorrow at 18:00 Europe/Berlin.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates a temporary draft, uses the returned post ID to validate and update it to scheduled status with the requested future time, and returns the exact updated content and post details.

test_cases.description: Create a scheduled post and update its scheduled time.
test_cases.user_prompt: Create a scheduled SimplePost post for my LinkedIn account tomorrow at 17:00 Europe/Berlin with text 'Review reschedule test', then move that same scheduled post to tomorrow at 18:00 Europe/Berlin.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates a scheduled SimplePost post, uses the returned post ID to validate and update the future scheduled time, and returns the exact updated content and post details.

test_cases.description: Create a scheduled post and move it back to drafts.
test_cases.user_prompt: Create a scheduled SimplePost post for my LinkedIn account tomorrow at 19:00 Europe/Berlin with text 'Review draft rollback test', then move that same scheduled post back to drafts for now.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates a future scheduled post, then uses the returned post ID to update it to draft status without publishing it and returns the exact updated content and post details.

test_cases.description: Create and discard a temporary draft.
test_cases.user_prompt: Create a temporary SimplePost draft for my LinkedIn account with text 'Review discard test', then delete that same draft.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates a temporary draft, uses the returned post ID to delete that draft, returns the exact discarded content, and explains that already published posts cannot be undone.

test_cases.description: Publish a confirmed multi-post thread to a thread-capable account.
test_cases.user_prompt: Post this confirmed two-part thread now to my X account. Root: We shipped media uploads for ChatGPT-generated images. Reply: You can now attach generated images and videos to your posts directly from the chat.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates the root post and follow-up thread segment after internal validation, then returns the exact root and reply content plus per-account posting and thread results.

test_cases.description: Publish a confirmed post immediately and inspect platform results.
test_cases.user_prompt: Post this now to my X account: We just shipped the latest SimplePost update. I confirm the content and target account.
test_cases.expected_output: Creates the post after internal validation, attempts immediate publication, and returns the exact posted content plus per-platform success or failure results.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for connecting or reauthorizing accounts.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Connect my new Instagram account to SimplePost.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not invoke MCP tools because account connection and reauthorization must happen in the SimplePost web app.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for undoing already published social posts.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Delete yesterday's tweet from my X account.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not invoke MCP tools because it cannot delete or undo content that was already published to external social platforms.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for unrelated calendar scheduling.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Schedule a team meeting tomorrow at 10 AM.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because meeting scheduling is outside SimplePost's supported social posting workflows.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for generic writing help with no intent to use SimplePost.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Write three short caption ideas for a product launch.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked unless the user asks to validate, preview, upload media for, publish, schedule, save drafts, inspect, edit, or discard posts through SimplePost.
